<!-- generated by sofar — do not hand-edit; truth lives in events.jsonl -->

# Decisions: notes-in-digest

- 2026-07-11T10:45:02.292Z — chose **Notes selection rule (pre-registered, 1.1): surface notes appended AFTER the last session_ended (the correction window), or ALL notes when nothing ever wrote back; carried on state.freshness as {ts, text}[] in log order, uncapped at fold; renderStatus count-caps + budget-clips, renderFullStatus renders the selection uncapped** over (a) last-K notes overall regardless of write-back boundary; (b) both windows with separate caps because Since-write-back IS the incident shape: every selected note postdates the summary/next_action the reader is about to trust, so each is un-absorbed by construction; it shares the exact reset event (session_ended) and lifecycle with freshness counters — staleness says THAT the record drifted, this shows WHAT — and it is self-clearing, so the section cannot accumulate (compaction-proof by construction, not cap tuning). Last-K loses: no natural clearing — an absorbed note re-renders forever, spending digest budget on stale content, and K misfires both ways (K absorbed notes render while the K+1th unabsorbed correction vanishes); it also makes notes a competing durable-memory channel with repo.md/decisions. Both-with-caps loses: two note sections double budget spend and force the reader to reconcile overlapping lists; old notes remain reachable via get_state view:full and the log. Accepted edge (consistent with freshness counter semantics): a note landing before an in-flight write-back counts as absorbed even if the ending agent never saw it — same boundary rule as the staleness counters, and the next SessionStart re-orients anyway.
